methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nox Metals is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zane Hengsperger that has raised $5.1M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including firms such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation profiles to support a reliable peer-median approach.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and provides the primary quantitative anchor. Using total raised ($5.1M) as a proxy for current implied value and applying a standard 2.5x multiplier for progression from seed to the next stage yields an implied valuation of $13M. This method reflects typical step-up dynamics observed in early-stage venture rounds within the sector.

The Peer-Median approach was not feasible due to insufficient narrow-set comparables; the broader sector peer universe of 11 firms does not yield three or more datapoints meeting stage, timing, or tier filters, which would render a median misleading. Consequently, the analysis relies on a single triangulation method. The supported valuation range is $11M to $15M, with a median of $13M. Key uncertainties include the absence of proximate peer validation, early stage maturity, and absence of revenue-based anchors. The range reflects stage-appropriate valuation progression absent more granular market data.
